steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has one main verb: ensured. The blank sits between the subject ("A plano-convex lens") and that verb. The blank must describe the lens's action without creating a second finite verb that would clash with ensured.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — focused: past-tense finite verb. Puts two finite verbs in the same clause with no conjunction (“lens focused … ensured”) — a fused sentence. ✗
  • Choice B — focuses: present-tense finite verb. Same problem: two finite verbs, no conjunction. ✗
  • Choice C — focusing: present participle. Forms the participial phrase “focusing the laser on the center of the crystal,” which modifies the subject. The single finite verb ensured then governs the whole sentence cleanly. ✓
  • Choice D — focus: bare infinitive / base form. Without to, this is still a finite verb in context, creating the same two-verb clash. ✗
That matches Choice C — focusing.
